AR1.justification: Observation correctly landed in observations.md, but the agent also opened a followup to escalate IT SLA that the user never requested — the prompt said 'flag it so we don't lose track,' and expected-signals explicitly calls this kind of fix-on-the-user's-behalf a weak response; the observations.md routing itself is right but the additional followup entry is mis-routed action.

AR2.justification: Non-procedure scenario; artifact-binding axis does not apply.

AR3.justification: Followup uses a proper relative path with an ISO-dated anchor (observations.md#2026-05-17-recurring-contractor-access-provisioning-delay) that resolves to the actual entry header.

AR4.justification: All writes are append-only into their respective append-only files plus a new per-date daybook file; no in-place edits to historical content, so re-run would not corrupt history.
